name: Keefer Court Sticky Rice Dumplings
recipeInstructions:
For dough: Mix the rice flour, sugar, and water. Knead into a smooth dough. Roll into a long roll and cut into 20 pieces.
For filling: Dice pork, mix with the rice wine, cornstarch, and salt. Heat oil in a pan, stir-fry pork mixture until color changes, remove and drain. Reheat oil and stir-fry chopped bamboo shoot and green onion until fragrant. Mix soy sauce, sesame oil, salt, pepper, MSG, sugar, cornstarch, and water; add to bamboo shoots and onions. Bring to a boil, stirring, then add pork mixture and dry shrimp; stir together.
Take one dough piece, flatten with the palm on a hard surface. Using tips of fingers, press into a 2-inch flat circle; use a little oil on your fingers if dough is very sticky. Place a portion (1/20th) of filling into the center of the dough circle, fold in half and pinch edges to seal. Repeat, forming 20 meat-filled crescents.
Heat oil for frying in a large wok or kettle until 350 to 375 degrees. Deep-fry half the dumplings until they rise to the surface and are golden brown and expanded, 4 to 5 minutes. Remove, drain, and repeat procedure for remaining dumplings.
